page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 30977.29 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12846.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 126.91 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 58.65 GB/sec for large block write operations. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, achieving 266.46 tokens/sec, while maintaining average speeds of 19.05 tokens/sec for medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7.8xlarge offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory arrays or local NVMe storage. The hardware configuration is well-suited for tasks such as web serving, software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
